differentials.name: Pars interarticularis defect: SPECT imaging adds sensitivity
differentials.raw: Pars interarticularis defect: SPECT imaging adds sensitivity

differentials.name: Osteoid osteoma: can be used intraoperatively to ensure removal of nidus
differentials.raw: Osteoid osteoma: can be used intraoperatively to ensure removal of nidus

differentials.name: < 10% 99mTc-tin colloid / free 99mTc-pertechnetate
differentials.raw: < 10% 99mTc-tin colloid / free 99mTc-pertechnetate (a good preparation is 95% bound)

differentials.name: Agent should not be used prior to 30 min after preparation
differentials.raw: Agent should not be used prior to 30 min after preparation

differentials.name: Avoid injection of air in preparation of multidose vials
differentials.raw: Avoid injection of air in preparation of multidose vials (oxidation results in poor Tc bond)

differentials.name: Kit life is 4-5 hours after preparation
differentials.raw: Kit life is 4-5 hours after preparation

context: Pediatric Dose Formula:   0.25 mCi/kg 99mTc-medronate; (minimum dose of 1 mCi) or dose range:   [(age + 1)/(age + 7)] × [lowest adult range] Imaging with Bone Agents @   Bone: 2-3 hours post injection ◊  Fractures may not show positive uptake until 3-10 days depending on age of patient @   Myocardium: 90-120 min post injection ◊  Ideal imaging time is 1-3 days post infarction Usual dose:   20 mCi (740 MBq) Radiation dose:   0.13 rad/mCi for bladder (critical organ), 0.04 rad/mCi for bone, 0.01 rad/mCi for whole body Labeling:  Tc (VII) is eluted as a pertechnetate ion; chemical reduction with
